title: papaver rhoeas f. subintegrum
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. subintegrum, also known as the red poppy, is a subspecies of the common poppy plant (Papaver rhoeas) that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as meconic acid and protopine. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas f. subintegrum include relief from pain, inflammation, and anxiety, as well as its potential to aid in sleep and reduce stress. Papaver rhoeas f. subintegrum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusing oil, which are commonly used in herbal remedies for various health issues.
